rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body claims about /skills slash command, User/Project skill scoping, and auto-discovery match shipped Claude Code reality (compendium §6 + §37 context-loading tiers).
/skills slash command + User/Project listing match documented skill-discovery model; no incorrect shortcut or path is asserted.
Scratch-path block correctly names $TMPDIR / /tmp / equivalent OS temp; kept-outside-repo framing consistent with the M4 durable-tier contrast.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source URL + type label mandatory on claims.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No practitioner citations, numbers, or external research claims in body — nothing to label.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks; open-hook when student input is genuinely required.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompts 2/4/5 use the open-hook pattern (single trailing colon line, student supplies the codebase-specific feature/surface). No [BRACKETS] inside any fence.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: One-sentence action lead-in for every prompt block.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Each of the five fences carries a verb-led semantic lead-in immediately above (list, invoke, walk through, integrate, add).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — `**Prompt** *(<dest>)*` with blank line + fence.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Expander reconstructs `**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*` for each of the five registry expansions.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed skills invoke by name, never by path.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompt 2 says `access-control-analysis skill` by name; no `content/skills/` or `~/.claude/skills/` path leaks inside any fence. Path appears only in maintainer-block plug-points, which is correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain by back-reference and deterministic path.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompt 3 back-references 'the path you told me'; prompt 4 'the surface-map file in the temp directory'; registry frontmatter binds produces/requires on access-surface-map. Deterministic enough — Claude picks the temp path and reports it back per prompt 2.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No CWD restatement inside the prompt body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No cd/path restatement inside any fence.
Registry expansion produces canonical `**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*` label.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts live in the registry; module/exercise files reference by marker.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All five prompts (1..5) exist at curriculum/prompts/map-the-access-surface-N.md with valid frontmatter (key/dest/runtime/origin/requires/produces); body uses {{prompt:<key>}} markers correctly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Declare permission-mode when paste requires a specific mode.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: None of these prompts require plan / acceptEdits / bypassPermissions; default mode is correct, no chip needed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism — every named tool/skill/path must exist at this point.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Verified: ~/.claude/skills/access-control-analysis/SKILL.md and ~/.claude/skills/stride/SKILL.md exist; source at content/skills/access-control-analysis/SKILL.md exists; prework Step 4 ships the install.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The 'delta' forcing function is encoded inside prompts 4 and 5 (integrate-the-hardened item; add-the-missed surface); body explains the contrast but does not carry the load-bearing mechanic.
Exercise does not build verification machinery; it invokes a curated skill. Smoke-test rule does not apply.
Phase 1 exposes the lazy-load tier via /skills slash command + prompt 1 (storage location + loaded-on-context status). Matches the tier-revealed-by-platform-surface principle.

platform_claims_checked.claim: /skills slash command lists installed skills with User/Project scoping
access-control-analysis skill auto-discovers by name when installed as personal skill
Skills authored later in Module 3 land in User scope
Claude picks a temp directory for the student's OS ($TMPDIR, /tmp, or equivalent)
